message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In this study, we propose and analyse an extended SEIARD model with vaccination. We compute the control reproduction number ℛ
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>c</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  of our model and study the stability of equilibria. We show that the set of disease-free equilibria is locally asymptotically stable when ℛ
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>c</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  < 1 and unstable when ℛ
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>c</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  > 1, and we provide a sufficient condition for its global stability. Furthermore, we perform numerical simulations using the reported data of COVID-19 infections and vaccination in Mexico to study the impact of different vaccination, transmission and efficacy rates on the dynamics of the disease.
                </jats:p>
title: An extended SEIARD model for COVID-19 vaccination in Mexico: analysis and forecast
